summary: Phase-2 Regelchunk: movement aus TSR Inc - AD&D 2nd Edition - Priest Spell Compendium Volume 3.pdf.

source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 133 | score=6] Whirlrhiin the whip, limbs trembling, and staggers at least IO feet away from the caster (unless the situation makes this movement impossible), hut is free to act nor- mally during the next round. The material component is a human hair that has been anointed with a drop of holy water on a consecrated altar of Beshaha. Notes: Granted by the evil goddess Beshaba, Maid of Misfortune, of the FORGOTTEN REALMS set- ting. This spell is found only in a few exception- ally rare tomes. Whirlchain (InvocationlEvocation) Sphere: Combat Level: 4 Range: 90 yds. Components: Casting Time: 7 Duration: 1 rd. Area of Effect: 1 creature Saving Throw: % This spell causes a helixlike length of chain to materializ
[PDF_PAGE 10 | score=5] Spike Growth Without the use of a spell such as true seeing, similar magical aids, or some other special means of detection (such as detect trans or detect snares and (Alteration, Enchantment) Sphere: Plant Level: 3 Range: 60 yds. Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 6 Duration: 3d4 turns + 1 tudevel Area of Effect: 10-ft. squarellevel Saving Throw: None Wherever any type of plant growth of moderate sue or density is found this spell can be used. The ground-covering vegetation or roots and rootlets in the area become very hard and sharply pointed. In effect the ground cover, while appearing to he unchanged acts as if the area were strewn with cal- trops. In areas of bare ground or earthen pits,
[PDF_PAGE 200 | score=4] Tanglefoot \ c Shadow Engines (IllusionPhantasm) Sphere: War Level: 7 Range: 240 yards Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 3 tuns Duration: 8 turns Area of Effect: 180-yard X 180-yard square Saving Throw: None This spell creates the illusion of as many as four siege engines. The casting priest can choose from hallistae, siege towers, catapults, rams, or any com- bination thereof. Like the creatures created hy the spell shudow monsters, these illusory engines have at least a tenuous reality and can inflict damage on enemies. Illusory crews of the appropriate number and race accompany the shadow engines. The engines can move at a rate of 20 yards per turn and are unaf- fected by terrain consider
[PDF_PAGE 196 | score=3] Gravity Variation Fortify (Alteration) Sphere: War Level: 3 Range: 60 yds. Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 1 rd. Duration: Instantaneous Area ofEffect: IO-ft. cuhe/2 levels Saving Throw: None By means of this spell, the priest prepares an area as a defensive position. The fortify spell can be used to prepare an open outdoors area such as a field, road, or grassland, or a rough or broken out- dwrs area such as a hillside, forest, or boulder-fall. Large rooms or chambers such as a cavern or a great hall can he fortified as well. The exact effects of the spell depend on the nature ofthe site. A. Open Outdoor Site: A rampart or dike of earth and loose stone rises from the ground along the peri
[PDF_PAGE 55 | score=2] Sylvan Form - Elf (Alteration) Sphere: Animal Level: 4 Range: 0 Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 7 Duration: 2 turnsllevel Area of Effect The caster Saving Throw: None When this spell is cast, the priest is able to assume the form of any non-evil humaniform sylvan creature, as adjudicated by the DM, from as small as a gorse (3 inches tall) to as large as a voad- kqn (9% feet tall). Other commonly assumed forms include that of an atamie, brownie, dobie, dryad, gig, hamadryad, kilmoulis, korred, leprechaun, nixie, nymph, pixie, satyr, sea sprite, seelie faerie, sprite, or sylph. It is not possible to assume nonhu- maniform guises, such as that of a centaur or mi- corn, nor that of an evil syl
[PDF_PAGE 84 | score=2] Tree Steed Tree Spirit (Necromancy) Sphere: Plant Level: 7 Range: Touch Components: Y S CastingTime: 1 turn Duration: Instantaneous Area of Effect: 1 tree Saving Throw: None Tree spirif permanently links the soul of the caster with a tree, usually chosen carefully for its health, vigor, and remote environment. Casting this spell joins the life force of the druid with that of the tree; as long as the tree lives, the caster ages atone- tenth the normal rate. (Because the spell causes the tree to devote all its energy to maintaining health rather than growth, it always remains exactly the size it was at the time of casting.) Moreover, the caster’s spirit merges with the tree at the charac- ter’
[PDF_PAGE 110 | score=2] > Wall ofThorns f .. the caster-and others whom they escort, while touching-to pass through the wall unharmed); SZ H-G (140 sq. ft. minimum); ML fearless (20); Int non- (0); AL N. Beuk Poison: Onset time 2d4 rounds; half ability scores and movement, no normal or mag- ical healing: effects last 3d4 days. If the wall is attacked by any spell or spell- like effect or is reduced to 99 or fewer hit points, it creates dadmess, li'radiirs from its outside surface, and bites any creatures it can reach. (20); Int non- (0); AL N. Beuk Poison: Onset time 2d4 rounds; half ability scores and movement, no normal or mag- ical healing: effects last 3d4 days. If the wall is attacked by any spell or spell- li
[PDF_PAGE 118 | score=2] permit spellcasting en route and provides no pro- tection for the caster at the end of the movement. (When the serpent recoils and collapses, it ends the spell and leaves the caster moving forward with the same momentum as the thrust, and, perhaps, noth- ing beneath him or her.) Such a thtut is made at MV 18, and can proj- ect beyond the body of water out of which the ser- pent was created. It is typically used to deposit the caster on a balcony, atop a wall, or on some other height clear of the water. If the creator of a water serpent wills the spell to en4 or the serpent passes beyond spell range, it instantly melts away, slumping hack into the snr- rounding waters as the magic dissipates.
